spatial_intelligence.title: What is spatial intelligence?
spatial_intelligence.summary: Spatial intelligence is how machines understand and reason about the 3D world: where things are, how they relate, and what happens when agents move through space. It sits at the intersection of computer vision, robotics, and AI.

pillars.name: 3D perception
pillars.desc: Reconstructing scenes from cameras and sensors: depth, point clouds, NeRFs, Gaussian splatting, and SLAM.

pillars.name: World models
pillars.desc: Learned simulators that predict how environments evolve. Used to train robot policies without endless real-world trials.

pillars.name: Spatial reasoning
pillars.desc: Answering questions like "what is behind that object?" or "can the arm reach that grasp?" Requires geometric and semantic understanding together.

pillars.name: Embodied AI
pillars.desc: Intelligence grounded in a body that moves. Vision-language-action models (VLAs) that map what they see to what they do.

spatial_intelligence.why_it_matters: Robots that only follow scripts fail in messy real environments. Spatial intelligence is what lets systems generalize: a humanoid in a new kitchen, a drone in changing weather, a surgical arm navigating tissue it has never seen.

highlights: Humanoid race heating up: Figure, Tesla Optimus, 1X, Apptronik pushing toward factory deployment.
World models for robotics: NVIDIA Cosmos, Google DeepMind, Stanford and Berkeley labs training sim-to-real pipelines.
Defense and space robotics funding remains strong through DARPA and NASA programs.
Industrial robotics leaders: ABB, KUKA, Universal Robots driving warehouse and manufacturing automation.
Strong research in safe human-robot collaboration and EU AI Act shaping deployment rules.
Mobile manipulation startups in Germany and Nordic countries focus on logistics.
Fastest scaling in humanoid and quadruped production, with heavy government and manufacturing backing.
DJI ecosystem dominance in aerial robotics; Unitree and others pushing affordable legged platforms.
Spatial AI research accelerating at Tsinghua, Shanghai AI Lab, and industry labs tied to EV supply chains.
Decades of precision robotics in automotive and electronics now merging with AI perception stacks.
Soft robotics and caregiving robots remain national priorities amid aging demographics.
Hyundai-Boston Dynamics integration signals Korean industrial push into mobile manipulation.

trends.title: Humanoids go commercial
trends.desc: 2025–2026 is the shift from demo videos to pilot factory programs. The bottleneck is no longer walking. It is reliable manipulation and cost.

trends.title: World models replace hand-built sims
trends.desc: Teams train policies inside learned video simulators instead of painstakingly modeling every object. Spatial intelligence makes this possible.

trends.title: VLA models unify see and act
trends.desc: Vision-language-action models like RT-2, OpenVLA, and pi0 map pixels and language directly to motor commands, blurring the line between perception and control.

trends.title: Physical AI funding wave
trends.desc: Investors are betting on "AI that touches the world": industrial autonomy, surgical robots, and construction automation seeing record rounds.
